hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in Information Technology Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. NAAC A+, NIRF #22. QS ranked. WES recognised.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in IT Management suits software engineers and IT professionals targeting product management, IT consulting, or technology leadership roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 at Rs 1,99,000. Bridges technical background with management credential. Sem 3-4 spec subjects confirmed each cycle.

about.body:
Information Technology Management is one of the most sought-after MBA specialisations among software engineers, developers, and IT professionals in India. The Amity Online MBA in IT Management bridges the technical-management gap, building capability in IT strategy, project management, ERP systems, IT governance, cybersecurity management, and digital transformation leadership.

The specialisation targets two audiences. The first is IT professionals with 2-7 years of technical experience who want to transition into product management, IT consulting, or technology leadership without relocating for a full-time programme. The second is non-technical managers in IT companies who want to formalise their understanding of technology systems and IT governance.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #22 University ranking for 2025. The IT Management MBA degree carries the Amity University name, identical to the campus degree. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from Amity. Full programme details at /universities/amity-university-online/mba.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Amity MBA specialisations. See the main Amity MBA page for core subjects.
syllabus.emptyFallback:
Semesters 1 and 2 of the Amity Online MBA cover the management core: Accounting for Managers, Managerial Economics, Marketing Management, Statistics for Management, Professional Communication, Business Research Methods, Financial Management, Human Resource Management, Legal Aspects of Business, and Conflict Resolution and Management.

Semesters 3 and 4 cover the Information Technology Management specialisation subjects. These typically include areas such as IT strategy and governance, project management and agile methodologies, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, cybersecurity management and IT risk, cloud computing management, IT procurement and vendor management, digital transformation strategy, and data management. The specific Sem 3 and Sem 4 subjects for Information Technology Management are confirmed by Amity each admission cycle. Our counsellor can share the current spec-level syllabus within 24 hours.

whoHires.body:
IT Management MBA graduates from Amity are hired across IT services, product companies, consulting, BFSI technology divisions, and enterprise software companies. Entry-level management roles (0-3 years post-MBA): Product Analyst, IT Project Coordinator, Business Analyst, ERP Implementation Consultant, and IT Operations Manager. These positions are common at mid-size IT services firms, SaaS companies, and IT consulting boutiques.

Mid-level roles (3-7 years): Product Manager, IT Project Manager, Technology Manager, Digital Transformation Consultant, and IT Governance Manager. Senior roles include CTO at mid-size companies, VP Technology, IT Director, and Chief Digital Officer.

Key hiring organisations include IT services majors (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, Tech Mahindra), consulting firms (Accenture, Capgemini, IBM), product companies (Zoho, Freshworks, Razorpay, BYJU's), technology arms of banks (HDFC Digital, Axis Technology), and large enterprise clients in manufacturing and logistics implementing ERP or digital transformation programmes.

skills.body:
The IT Management specialisation builds skills that technology hiring managers test at product manager and IT project manager interview stages:

IT Strategy and Governance: aligning IT investments with business objectives, IT audit basics, and COBIT/ITIL frameworks — tested at IT Manager and technology consulting roles. Project Management: Agile, Scrum, and PMI-aligned project management methodologies relevant to both product and IT delivery roles. ERP Systems: understanding SAP, Oracle, and Microsoft Dynamics architecture and implementation lifecycle — valued in consulting and enterprise client roles. Cybersecurity Management: risk frameworks, incident response planning, and vendor security assessment — increasingly tested even in non-security IT management roles. Digital Transformation: cloud adoption strategy, legacy modernisation, and technology change management for enterprise clients. Data Management: data governance, master data management, and basics of data architecture relevant to IT Manager roles overseeing analytics teams.

Soft skills developed include technology roadmap communication to non-technical stakeholders, vendor negotiation, and IT budget management.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA IT Management against peers:

Amity vs LPU IT Management MBA (/universities/lovely-professional-university-online/mba): LPU is NAAC A++ and NIRF #31. Fee is lower (around Rs 1,20,000). LPU's IT Management content is generally solid and its placement network is strong in IT sector. Amity's QS ranking and NIRF #22 give it an edge for MNC technology hiring and roles requiring a brand with global recognition.

Amity vs Chandigarh University IT Management MBA (/universities/chandigarh-university-online/mba): CU is NAAC A+ and NIRF #19. Similar fee. CU has excellent IT sector placement infrastructure given its large campus placement network. For roles in north India IT hubs (Chandigarh, Noida), CU may have a network advantage. Amity's brand is stronger at pan-India multinational IT companies.

Amity vs Manipal Online MBA (/universities/manipal-academy-higher-education-online/mba): MAHE Online is NAAC A++ and NIRF #6. Fees are significantly higher (around Rs 2.5-3 lakh). The Manipal-Coursera dual certification is a strong differentiator for IT professionals. If budget allows, MAHE Online IT Management carries stronger brand for senior IT management roles.

fees.body: The total fee for the Amity University Online MBA (all specialisations including Information Technology Management) is approximately Rs 1,99,000. This includes a one-time registration fee of Rs 2,500 and four semester fees of Rs 49,750 each. EMI options start from Rs 8,292 per month. Examination fees may apply separately. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees each quarter. Verify the current fee at the official Amity Online portal before paying. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name and the MBA designation. The specialisation (Information Technology Management) is noted on the marksheet and transcript. The certificate does not display the word online on the face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. For IT professionals using the degree in international job applications, note the WES recognition — the degree can be officially verified for employers in Canada and the US.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA IT Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Deepak Sharma
items.liked: IT strategy and digital transformation content was directly applicable to my consulting work
items.disliked: Agile content was basic for someone already Scrum certified — could have been more advanced
items.body: I was a software developer looking to move into IT consulting. This MBA gave me the management framing I was missing. The digital transformation strategy module was taught by a practitioner and was genuinely current. Got a Business Analyst role at a Big Four consulting firm within 6 months. Amity brand was specifically mentioned positively in the interview.

items.name: Meera Pillai
items.liked: ERP implementation module covered SAP architecture in enough depth for my work context
items.disliked: Cybersecurity content needed more depth — covered only frameworks, not practical tooling
items.body: I was an SAP functional consultant wanting a management degree. The ERP module was actually useful — not generic. Got a Technology Manager role managing an SAP implementation at a manufacturing company. The MBA gave me the credibility to be taken seriously in management meetings that were previously dominated by MBAs from campus programmes.

items.name: Rajesh Kumar
items.liked: IT governance and vendor management content was practical for my IT Manager responsibilities
items.disliked: Cloud computing management section needed AWS and Azure specific content rather than generic theory
items.body: I manage a team of 12 in IT infrastructure at a mid-size company. This MBA helped me understand IT governance frameworks I had been applying intuitively. Got promoted to IT Head after completing Year 2. The Amity NIRF #22 ranking made the degree credible when HR reviewed my file for the promotion.

faqs.question: Is Amity MBA IT Management equivalent to an MCA for IT sector hiring?
faqs.answer: No. MBA IT Management is a management degree; MCA is a technical degree. For management and product roles in IT companies, the MBA is often preferred. For software development and technical architecture roles, an MCA or B.Tech is more relevant. The MBA IT Management is best suited for IT professionals transitioning into management, consulting, or product roles.

faqs.question: Does this specialisation prepare for PMP or PRINCE2 certification?
faqs.answer: The project management module covers PMI-aligned frameworks and is conceptually aligned with PMP preparation. However, the MBA does not provide PMP certification itself. Candidates should pursue PMP separately after the degree. The MBA provides the management foundation; PMP certification adds the globally recognised PM credential for project management roles.

faqs.question: What is the salary jump expected for IT professionals after this MBA?
faqs.answer: Software engineers and IT professionals with 3-5 years experience typically see 25-45% salary jumps when transitioning to product analyst, IT project manager, or business analyst roles post-MBA. Senior IT professionals transitioning to IT Management or Director roles see larger jumps. Salary outcomes are strongly dependent on pre-MBA experience and target role.

faqs.question: Does the IT Management MBA include programming or technical coursework?
faqs.answer: No. The MBA IT Management is a management degree, not a technical programme. It does not include programming, software development, or data science coursework. Technical skills remain the candidate's responsibility. The degree builds management, strategy, and governance skills layered on top of existing technical expertise.

faqs.question: Can I do this MBA while working full-time as a software engineer?
faqs.answer: Yes. Live sessions are on weekends with recorded lectures available 24/7. Online proctored examinations mean no campus visits are required. The workload is designed for working professionals managing 40-50 hour work weeks. Most IT professionals find the weekend schedule manageable alongside their primary role.

chooseIf: You are an IT professional with 2+ years of technical experience wanting to move into product management, IT consulting, or technology leadership.
You want a NIRF #22, NAAC A+ management credential at under Rs 2 lakh to complement your technical qualifications.
You are targeting IT Manager, Product Analyst, or Digital Transformation roles at large IT companies or MNC clients.
consider: You are targeting senior technology leadership at premium companies — MAHE Online or Jain University Online with Coursera dual certifications offer stronger brand for those roles.
You want data science or AI content alongside management — the Amity Business Analytics or Data Science spec is more appropriate.
You are a non-IT professional wanting to understand technology — General Management with elective technology modules may be a better fit than a dedicated IT Management spec.
